Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

CTN 4, 127 [Namburbu][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] ⸢ḪUL⸣-šu? ḪUL ⸢KI?⸣ ŠEŠ E₂-šu₂ x x A

(o 1) [...] his evil, the evil of this place, his house, ....

o 22

DU₃.DU₃-[BI ... A]-MEŠ KU₃-⸢MEŠ⸣ SU₃ ana de₂-a dUTU

(o 2) Its ritual: [...]. You sprinkle pure water. [...] for Ea, Šamaš and Asalluhi. You strew dates (and) fine flour.

o 33

u ⸢d⸣[ASAR.LU₂.ḪI ...] ZU₂.LUM.MA ⸢zi₃⸣EŠA DUBaq

o 44

⸢NINDA⸣.[I₃.DE₂.A ... GAR]an dugA.DA.GUR₄ GUBan

(o 4) You put down a cake (made) of [...]. You set up a vessel for libations.

o 55

[...] U₂ gišKIRI₆ ta-tab-bak!(EN)

(o 5) [...] you pile up plants from the orchard.

o 66

[... šid]-⸢di⸣ GID₂⸢ad ZI₃.DUB⸣.DUB.BU-x? ŠUB

(o 6) [...]. You draw the curtains. You lay down a small heap of flour.

o 77

[x] BI ⸢EGIR⸣ [...] x [... DU₁₁].⸢GA⸣

(o 7) That [...] behind the [...] ... he speaks (as follows).

o 88

[x] de₂-a d⸢UTU⸣ u d⸢ASAR⸣.[LU₂.ḪI ... mu]-⸢šim-mu NAM⸣-MEŠ

(o 8) [...]. Ea, Šamaš and Asalluhi, ..., the ones who decree destinies!

o 99

[mu-ṣi]-⸢ru⸣ [GIŠ].⸢ḪUR⸣ x [... da]-⸢nu₃⸣

(o 9) The designers of plans, ... [...] Anu!

o 1010

[x x] ⸢TI.LA⸣ [...]

(o 10) [...] of life.

o 1111

GIŠ.⸢ḪUR⸣-MEŠ ⸢TI.LA⸣ [... tu-uṣ-ṣa]-⸢ra⸣

(o 11) [...] you draw the plans of life.

o 1212

⸢ṣi⸣-it pi-⸢ku⸣-nu TI.⸢LA⸣ [... TI].⸢LA⸣-[ma]

(o 12) The utterance of your mouths is life, the [...] is life.

o 1313

ana-ku ARAD-⸢ku⸣-nu as-ḫur-[ku-nu-ši ...]

(o 13) I, your servant, have sought you, [...].

o 1414

u₂-kab-bit-ku-nu-ši [...]

(o 14) I have honoured you.

(o 14) [...] I am frightened and I am afraid [...].

o 1515

⸢pal⸣-ḫa-ku-ma ad-[ra-ku ...]

o 1616

⸢ḪUL⸣ BI a-a ⸢TEa⸣ [...]

(o 16) May that evil not approach, [...].

o 1717

⸢li⸣-bir ID₂ ⸢li⸣-[bal-kit ...]

(o 17) May it go across the watercourse, may it cross over the [...].

o 1818

⸢GIM⸣ qut-ri li-⸢tel⸣-[li ...]

(o 18) May it go up like smoke [...].

Reverse
r 1r 1

gišŠINIG KU₃-⸢an⸣-ni u₂⸢IN⸣.NU.UŠ BUR₂-an-[ni]

(r 1) May the tamarisk purify me, may the maštakal-plant release me.

r 22

KItu₄ lim-ḫur-an-⸢ni KI⸣tu₄ lip-danan-⸢ni⸣ lid-di-na me-⸢lam⸣-[ma-ša₂-ma]

(r 2) May the earth receive (the evil) from me, may the earth set me free, may it give me its fearsome radiance.

r 33

u₄-mu šul-⸢mu ITI⸣ ḪUL₂ [x] ḪE₂.GAL₂la-[ša₂] lib-[lam-ma]

(r 3) May the day bring me health, the month joy, the [...] its plenty, and may the day (and) the sun take away my evils from it.

r 44

u₄-mu dUTU ⸢ina? ŠA₃?⸣ lit-⸢ba⸣-[lu] ⸢lum?⸣-ni-[ia]

r 55

GIM an-nam ⸢3⸣-[šu₂ x x] dUTU ⸢ŠID?⸣[nu]-u₂ ana IGI ID₂ UR₅.⸢GIM⸣ [x x]

(r 5) When he has recited this 3 times [...] Šamaš, [...] in front of the river.

r 66

EN₂ at-ti ⸢ID₂⸣ DU₃⸢at DU₃-ma⸣ [e]-nu-ma ⸢iḫ⸣-ru-ki DINGIR-MEŠ ⸢GAL⸣-[MEŠ]

(r 6) Incantation. You, river, the creator of everything, when the great gods excavated you, they placed bounty on your banks. Ea established his dwelling [...] your midst.

r 77

ina ⸢A₂-ki iš-ku-na⸣ dum-⸢qa⸣ [x] ⸢ŠA₃⸣-ki ⸢dIDIM?⸣ ir-ma-a šu-⸢bat⸣-[su]

r 88

⸢iš-ruk⸣-ki-ma uz-za ⸢na-mur-ra-ta⸣ pu-luḫ-⸢tu₂⸣

(r 8) He gave you wrath, awe-inspiring radiance and fearsomeness.

r 99

⸢a⸣-bu-ub la ma-⸢ḫar⸣ [MU]-ki im-bi

(r 9) He gave (you) your name Irresistible Flood.

r 1010

dEN.⸢BI⸣.LU.LU GU₂.GALla-⸢ki⸣ [u₂]-⸢kal⸣ rik-si-ki

(r 10) Enbilulu, your irrigation controller, maintains your organisation.

r 1111

ga-mi-ra-ti ma-⸢ḫar-ki di⸣-[ni] ⸢ID₂⸣ GALti ID₂ MAḪti

(r 11) You are decisive in the legal decisions (that come) before you. River you are great, river you are exalted.

r 1212

ID₂ eš-re-e-ti šu-[šu]-ru ⸢A-MEŠ⸣-ki

(r 12) River, you are just, your waters are in order.

r 1313

muḫ-ri-ni-ma ḪUL ⸢lu₂?⸣TUR [x x] ⸢iti⸣BARA₂ ⸢U₃.TU⸣

(r 13) Receive from me the evil of the child [...] was born [...] (the month of) Nisannu.

r 1414

pal-ḫa-ku-ma ad-ra-⸢ku⸣ [x] ⸢šu⸣-[ta-du]-⸢ra⸣-ku ID₂ lim-ḫur-an-ni ID₂ lip-danan-ni

(r 14) I am frightened and I am afraid [...] I am constantly terrified. May the river receive (the evil) from me, may the river set me free.

r 1515

lim-ḫur me-ḫer-ta ša [... li]-še-rid lib-bu-uš-ša

(r 15) May it recieve the produce of [...], may it send (it) downstream within it.

r 1616

[x] ⸢ḪA? A?⸣ [x] ⸢du-muq⸣-ki [lu]-⸢mur⸣ U₄-⸢MEŠ⸣-ia ur-ri-ki TI.LA!(AD) šur-ki

(r 16) [...] ... [...] may I see your bounty, lengthen my days (and) give me (long) life.

r 1717

[...] IGI ID₂ ŠID⸢nu⸣-[ma x] E₂-šu₂ [SI].⸢SA₂ ḪUL-šu? DU₈⸣

(r 17) He recites [...] front of the river and goes straight [...] his house. His evil will be released.
